wordpress.txt:
- WordPress is a free system for building websites and blogs.
- It started as a blog tool but now makes many types of websites.
- Users can change a site's design using themes and add features with plugins.
- It was created in 2003 and is very popular for building websites.
- To use it, you need to install it on a web server or hosting service.
OSM.txt:
- OpenStreetMap is a free map database created by volunteers.
- It started in 2004 because UK map data was not freely available.
- The project is supported by a non-profit foundation funded by donations.
- Many companies now use its data, especially after Google Maps introduced fees.
- The map stores locations using points, lines, and grouped relationships.
wayback_machine.txt:
- It is a free online archive of old websites.
- It was launched to the public in 2001.
- It saves copies of web pages as they change over time.
- Users can view how a website looked on a past date.
- It has archived over a trillion web pages.
wikipedia.txt:
- Wikipedia is a free online encyclopedia created by volunteers.
- It started in 2001 and is now available in over 340 languages.
- It is the largest and most-read reference work in history.
- The site is funded by donations and hosted by a nonprofit foundation.
- It has been both praised for its knowledge and criticized for biases.
chatoyancy.txt:
- Chatoyancy is a visual effect in some gems that looks like a moving band of light.
- This effect is often called "cat's eye" because it resembles a cat's eye.
- It is caused by tiny, aligned fibers or structures inside the stone.
- The effect is best seen when the gem is cut into a smooth, rounded shape.
- The phenomenon can also occur in certain types of wood and other materials.
youtube.txt:
- YouTube is a popular video website founded in 2005 by three former PayPal employees.
- Google bought YouTube in 2006, and it now earns money from ads and paid subscriptions.
- The site hosts billions of videos, with users uploading hundreds of hours of new content every minute.
- It started from an idea to share videos easily and its first clip was uploaded in April 2005.
- The platform's rapid growth was helped by viral videos, but it has also faced criticism over various issues.
8086.txt:
- The Intel 8086 was a 16-bit computer chip released in 1978.
- It started the x86 family, which is still used in computers today.
- It was designed to be similar to older Intel 8-bit chips.
- This made it easier for older programs to work on it.
- The chip's design was created quickly by a small team.
c_lang.txt:
- C is a programming language created in the 1970s by Dennis Ritchie.
- It is widely used for operating systems and embedded devices.
- The language provides direct access to computer hardware and memory.
- C programs can run on many different types of computers with few changes.
- It remains one of the most popular programming languages.
annas_arch.txt:
- It is a search engine for free online book and article collections.
- It gathers records from other large free library websites.
- The site does not host files but provides links to download them.
- It aims to preserve and provide access to all books digitally.
- The project faces legal challenges for linking to copyrighted material.
chatgpt.txt:
- ChatGPT is an AI chatbot that responds to text, audio, and image prompts.
- It gained 100 million users quickly and is among the world's top websites.
- The chatbot can write, translate, and code but sometimes gives wrong answers.
- It learns from human feedback and data like Wikipedia to improve its responses.
- New features allow it to search the web, make phone calls, and act as a web browser.
